ruleset.name: Best Practices
ruleset.description: Rules which enforce generally accepted best practices.

rule.name: AvoidMessageDigestField
rule.message: MessageDigest fields are not thread-safe; declare a local instance instead.
rule.description:
java.security.MessageDigest is not thread-safe. Declaring it as a class field exposes it to concurrent access without explicit synchronisation, producing incorrect digests. Create a new instance per method call, or protect shared access with a mutex.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/bestpractices.xml/AvoidMessageDigestField

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidPrintStackTrace
rule.message: Avoid printStackTrace(); use a logger call instead.
rule.description:
Avoid calling Throwable.printStackTrace(); use a proper logger (e.g. SLF4J, Log4j2) instead. Stack traces printed to standard output can be lost in production and carry no log level, making them hard to control or suppress.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/bestpractices.xml/AvoidPrintStackTrace

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidStringBufferField
rule.message: StringBuffer/StringBuilder fields can grow large and become a source of memory leaks.
rule.description:
StringBuffer and StringBuilder instances can grow considerably and, if held as class fields across long-lived objects, may cause memory leaks. Prefer creating a local instance inside the method that needs it, or use String concatenation / string templates which the Kotlin compiler optimises automatically.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/bestpractices.xml/AvoidStringBufferField

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: FunctionNameTooShort
rule.message: Function names should have non-cryptic and clear names.
rule.description: Function names should be easy to understand and describe the intention. Makes developers happy.
rule.example:
fun cl() {} // violation, no unavailable attribute added to the function declaration
fun calculateLayout() // no violation

rule.name: SystemPrintln
rule.message: Usage of System.out/err print methods; use a proper logger instead.
rule.description:
Calls to System.out.print*, System.err.print*, or System.out.printf are typically left-over debug statements. Use a proper logging framework (SLF4J, Log4j2, kotlin-logging) so output can be controlled by log level and configuration without redeploying.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/bestpractices.xml/SystemPrintln

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.
